tr.th: Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us

tr.td: The patient shows elevated serum creatinine, suggesting impaired renal function. Urinalysis reveals proteinuria, glucosuria, ketonuria, and bilirubinuria, with abnormal urine appearance and low specific gravity, indicating possible renal tubular dysfunction and/or acute metabolic disturbance. HbA1c is low, arguing against chronic hyperglycemia. Findings warrant further evaluation for acute kidney injury, possible hepatic involvement, and metabolic derangements. Clinical correlation is recommended.

display.value: Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
